title: Google Meet Quality Metrics
dataSources: Meet quality tool export / BigQuery (`conference_records`, participant `score` metrics)
spl:
index=gws sourcetype="gws:meet_quality"
| where packet_loss_pct > 2 OR round_trip_time_ms > 300 OR jitter_ms > 35
| stats avg(packet_loss_pct) as avg_loss, avg(round_trip_time_ms) as avg_rtt by conference_id, organizer_email
| sort -avg_loss
description: Meet quality telemetry (packet loss, jitter, RTT) for troubleshooting conferencing issues—extends UC-11.2.5 with org-level rollups.
value: Meet quality telemetry (packet loss, jitter, RTT) for troubleshooting conferencing issues—extends UC-11.2.5 with org-level rollups.
implementation: Enable Meet quality logging in Admin. Ingest via BigQuery export or Reports API. Baseline per office ASN. Alert when median MOS proxy metrics exceed SLA.
visualization: Table (worst conferences), Line chart (loss/jitter trend), Bar chart (by network location).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Meet quality tool export / BigQuery (`conference_records`, participant `score` metrics).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable Meet quality logging in Admin. Ingest via BigQuery export or Reports API. Baseline per office ASN. Alert when median MOS proxy metrics exceed SLA.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=gws sourcetype="gws:meet_quality"
| where packet_loss_pct > 2 OR round_trip_time_ms > 300 OR jitter_ms > 35
| stats avg(packet_loss_pct) as avg_loss, avg(round_trip_time_ms) as avg_rtt by conference_id, organizer_email
| sort -avg_loss
```

Understanding this SPL

**Google Meet Quality Metrics** — Meet quality telemetry (packet loss, jitter, RTT) for troubleshooting conferencing issues—extends UC-11.2.5 with org-level rollups.

Documented **Data sources**: Meet quality tool export / BigQuery (`conference_records`, participant `score` metrics). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: gws; **sourcetype**: gws:meet_quality. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=gws, sourcetype="gws:meet_quality". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where packet_loss_pct > 2 OR round_trip_time_ms > 300 OR jitter_ms > 35` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by conference_id, organizer_email** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Google Admin console audit and Google Workspace reports for the same org unit and time window.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (worst conferences), Line chart (loss/jitter trend), Bar chart (by network location).
grandmaExplanation: We watch Meet call quality, so we can tell a bad call from a bad day on the public Internet.
knownFalsePositives: ISP and Wi-Fi issues driving Meet scores down without any change in admin policy.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
